messages.content:
Important base-branch check before you finalize. Your worktree appears to have branched from an OLD commit (c683c5e) that is MISSING all of Phase A/B/D. Your corpus work depends on those changes: `cli/src/scanner/context.rs` (is_declaration_file, called from scan_code_patterns), `cli/src/scanner/normalize.rs`, the ignore/rayon walker in mod.rs, the single-file rel_path fix, and the exit-code contract in main.rs.

Before your final commit, run:
  git merge-base --is-ancestor e8d7265 HEAD && echo HAS_AB || echo MISSING_AB

If MISSING_AB: rebase your work onto `f-008/phase-a-b-hardening` (currently at c1de77b), i.e. `git rebase f-008/phase-a-b-hardening`, resolve conflicts in phases.rs/mod.rs (keep BOTH Phase B's context-suppression/normalize calls AND your corpus changes), then re-run `cargo test` — the full suite must pass (it now includes walker_tests, normalize::tests, context::tests, fixtures_tests, exit_code_tests = 23 baseline tests plus yours). Your parity golden-snapshot must be captured against the Phase-B baseline, not the old one, or it will encode the .d.ts false positives that Phase B already fixed.

If you've already rebased/ff'd (like the D3 agent did), ignore this. Report your final branch's merge-base status either way.
